C.disclaimer: This output is a structural similarity aid derived from final mixed audio only. It is not a legal proof of plagiarism or authorship.

lyrics_structure.normalized_text:
I saw a child draw borders

With a stick in the sand

Then the tide came laughing softly

And erased them from the land

A woman lit a candle

In a window full of rain

Said, “May the ones who hate me

Find their way back home again”


And all the guns are heavy

When the heart has had enough

Maybe peace begins in silence

Maybe silence turns to love


So lay your weapons down

Let the sky come closer

Let the broken cities breathe

Let the world get sober

We are more than flags

More than wounds we carry

With our hands open wide

We can still be ordinary
stanzas: I saw a child draw borders
With a stick in the sand
Then the tide came laughing softly
And erased them from the land
In a window full of rain
Said, “May the ones who hate me
Find their way back home again”
And all the guns are heavy
When the heart has had enough

lines.text: I saw a child draw borders
With a stick in the sand
Then the tide came laughing softly
And erased them from the land
In a window full of rain
Said, “May the ones who hate me
Find their way back home again”
And all the guns are heavy
When the heart has had enough

point_of_view_options: First-person as a bartender reflecting on patrons' stories
First-person as a lonely drinker reminiscing on past love
First-person as a friend trying to comfort a grieving companion
writing_brief.emotional_temperature: bittersweet sips of nostalgia and longing
possible_scenes: A dimly lit bar with clinking glasses, where the protagonist crafts mojitos while observing the world outside
A sun-drenched patio where a couple shares laughter and secrets over their drinks, now just a memory
An empty kitchen at midnight, where the protagonist mixes ingredients alone, the sound of ice clinking echoing the silence
promising_images: fresh mint leaves trembling in the breeze
a glass sweating under the heat of a long summer day
the sound of laughter fading into the night
chorus_concepts: A repetition of the act of making mojitos, mirroring the protagonist's attempts to reclaim lost moments, with a haunting refrain about the taste of solitude.
A contrast between the bright flavors of mojitos and the shadows of loneliness, using the drink as a metaphor for fleeting joy and lingering sorrow.
narrative_arc_options: A moment of joy at the bar -> a memory surfaces -> the realization of loss
Crafting a drink for a friend -> the friend’s absence becomes palpable -> finding solace in shared memories
Serving mojitos to couples -> feeling the weight of solitude -> a decision to reach out for connection
writing_brief.copyright_safe_note: AI-generated brief: abstract directions and original seed images only. No existing song is reproduced. The songwriter remains the author.

abstract_patterns.imagery_density: mid — balance of statement and image

creative_constraints: Aim for a chorus that is memorable repeated phrase.
Build verses that are regular lines establishing situation.
Target imagery density: mid — balance of statement and image.
References' real catalog leans toward moods: angst, love, reflection, despair.
Recurring themes in the references: spiritual connection, loneliness and seeking support, forgiveness and redemption, support and reassurance, loss and farewell.

generated_draft.title: Mojito Reflections

lines: a splash of something sweet, a hint of lime,
where laughter danced, the good times rhyme.
as echoes wrap around the glass so tight,
in every swirl, where shadows fight.
come drifting back with every clink of ice,
creating dreams that never quite suffice.
of moments lost in summer's heat and glow,
for memories that ebb and flow.
a taste of joy that’s bittersweet and bright,
the weight of what was shared beneath the light.
like ghosts of laughter fading into night,
the solitude that lingers in my sight.

generated_draft.notes: The song captures the bittersweet nostalgia of a bartender crafting mojitos, reflecting on lost connections and memories tied to the act of mixing drinks.
